clusters.title: Reuses an existing protobuf field number for a different wire type

member_findings.title: Reuses an existing protobuf field number for a different wire type
member_findings.impact: RegisterClientRspPb field 24 was previously an int32 log_rate_limit and is now a LogSampleConfigPb message. During mixed-version client/worker deployments, protobuf readers see the known field with the wrong wire type and drop it as unknown, so the worker sampling policy is silently not applied to clients.
member_findings.fix: Do not reuse field 24. Reserve the old log_rate_limit tag and add log_sample_config on a new tag, then update the sender/receiver and tests to use the new field number.

clusters.title: Avoid materializing a second request-message string for every access log

member_findings.title: Avoid materializing a second request-message string for every access log
member_findings.impact: LogPerformance now calls FormatAccessReqMsg(type, reqMsg) and streams the returned std::string. That helper builds a separate ostringstream for sampled request/access logs, and for REQUEST_OUT or unsampled cases it returns reqMsg by value, so hot access/request-out logging copies the whole formatted request payload before the outer logStream copies/appends it again. Large reqMsg values such as ShortDebugString() pay this extra allocation/copy on every emitted record.
member_findings.fix: Keep the previous append-style API: pass the destination std::ostringstream into FormatAccessReqMsg/AppendReqMsgWithSampleMark and write directly into the existing logStream, returning void or a string_view-only fast path instead of a new std::string.

clusters.title: Do not copy protobuf key lists before truncating access-log output

member_findings.title: Do not copy protobuf key lists before truncating access-log output
member_findings.impact: The PROTO_REF path reserves protoRef->size(), copies every protobuf string into a std::vector, then calls objectKeysToString(keys). For batched worker/object operations this adds O(n) string allocations and a second pass even though the formatter caps output around LOG_TOTAL_KEYS_SIZE_LIMIT/LOG_OBJECT_KEY_SIZE_LIMIT and can stop early.
member_findings.fix: Call the new objectKeysToString(const google::protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<std::string>&) overload directly from the PROTO_REF case, or make FillObjectKey iterate the protobuf field with the same early-exit truncation logic.

clusters.title: Access recorder facade repeats field plumbing across many setters

member_findings.title: Access recorder facade repeats field plumbing across many setters
member_findings.impact: The new facade adds dozens of near-identical setters, each repeating the sampling guard, state assignment, and return pattern, plus separate Fill and Record plumbing for each field. Future access-log fields will require synchronized edits across the header state structs, setter declarations, setter bodies, fill logic, and record assembly, which is easy to miss.
member_findings.fix: Factor the common guarded-assignment pattern into small helpers or a table-driven field descriptor so adding a field has one obvious implementation path and the object, stream, and request-out facades share the repeated mechanics.

clusters.title: Sampler snapshot publication is duplicated across config sources

member_findings.title: Sampler snapshot publication is duplicated across config sources
member_findings.impact: The proto path manually rebuilds SampleRate objects, allocates a LogSamplerSnapshot, exchanges it, tracks the old snapshot, and updates samplerEnabled, while the flags path has separate publication logic. The next sampling field or lifecycle rule has to be updated in multiple places, making config-source drift likely.
member_findings.fix: Move ppm-to-SampleRate construction and snapshot publication into one helper, for example PublishSnapshotFromPpm(reqPpm, accPpm, diagPpm), and call it from both UpdateConfigFromProto and the flags-derived path.

clusters.title: Heartbeat config refresh path lacks a regression test

member_findings.title: Heartbeat config refresh path lacks a regression test
member_findings.impact: The PR adds LogSampleConfigPb to heartbeat responses, and clients also apply heartbeat-delivered configs, but the new coverage only verifies proto parsing and initial register delivery. If a worker sampling policy changes after a client is already connected, a regression in heartbeat population or client application would leave the client on stale sampling rates without any test failing.
member_findings.fix: Add an end-to-end or component test that connects a client, changes the worker sampler config after registration, sends or waits for a heartbeat, and asserts the client LogSampler snapshot changes to the new request/access/diagnostic ppm values while invalid heartbeat configs retain the previous-good snapshot.
